inn-corner.speaker: narrator
inn-corner.text: He is at a small table near the hearth, the notebook open and a second smaller satchel at his feet. He has put on a heavier coat for the road. The pencil tucks itself away again as you cross the room. He stands when he sees you, then half-sits, then stands again — the small unrehearsed dance of a man who is unused to being expected.

halvor-greet.speaker: halvor
halvor-greet.text: You came. I had the satchel packed by noon and have been re-packing it ever since. There is no graceful way to admit that. Sit, please. There is one more thing I owe you before we speak of any road.

halvor-warning.speaker: halvor
halvor-warning.text: I will bring you danger. The thesis is not a thing one carries quietly. If I am right — and I am almost sure I am right — then the names I have written will start finding ways into the same conversations as you. The watchers in the deep do not like being read.

halvor-offer.speaker: halvor
halvor-offer.text: I will also bring you knowledge. I have herbs and bandages, and a tongue that has read the names of every wall we will walk past. I cannot ask gold for that — knowledge for hire is the kind of bargain my old tutors warned against. So I am offering it freely, for the road, for as long as you will keep me from being foolish on it.

choices.text: Walk with me. The road is yours.
Not tonight. Hold the satchel. I will return.

recruit.speaker: halvor
recruit.text: ...Aye. Then it is a road. Forgive me — I had a small speech prepared and forgot every word of it the moment you said yes. Let me say only this. I will not slow you. I will know what we are looking at. I will keep the bandages dry. That is the bargain.

recruit-done.speaker: halvor
recruit-done.text: One small thing. If we find a wall that hums, or a stone that does not feel like stone — let me read it before we strike it. A second of reading has saved a great many lives. I am hoping it will save ours, often, in the months ahead.

decline.speaker: halvor
decline.text: Of course. The satchel will keep. The notebook will keep. I will keep, for a little longer. Find me here when the road is decided. I will not pack again until you do. It saves wear on the strap.
